mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment above 7.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment above 7.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 98 signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production below 98 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.1 million signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts above 1.1 million are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real GDP below 13650 signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining real GDP below 13650 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in initial unemployment claims above 250 thousand signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising initial unemployment claims above 250 thousand are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 2.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate above 2.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real disposable personal income below 14000 signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining real disposable personal income below 14000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
